skill.name: claude-opus-4-5-migration
skill.description: Guides migrations from Claude Sonnet 4.0, Sonnet 4.5, and Opus 4.1 to Claude Opus 4.5. It helps update model strings, remove unsupported beta headers, configure effort settings, and adjust prompts only when users report specific Opus 4.5 behavior changes.
skill.summary: Migrate Claude model usage and prompts to Opus 4.5 with platform-specific guidance.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command-execution and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found markdown backticks, code examples, model identifiers, and prompt guidance rather than executable code. No malicious intent, network exfiltration, credential access, or auditor-directed prompt injection was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill guides user-approved edits to code and prompts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Syntax Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are false positives. The cited locations use markdown inline code, fenced examples, model strings, and API configuration examples, not runnable shell or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are markdown documentation and the flagged syntax appears inside prose, tables, or fenced examples. I found no script file, executable command invocation, or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The cited content discusses prompt behavior, frontend wording, model descriptions, and XML tags rather than hashing, encryption, or signature verification.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review of the cited lines shows documentation and prompt text only. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: Prompt Behavior Snippets Can Change Assistant Conduct
low_findings.description: The skill includes snippets intended for insertion into system prompts. This is expected for a prompt migration skill, but users should apply them only when needed because they can make assistant behavior more forceful or stylistically opinionated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The snippets are explicitly labeled as prompt additions and include directive language. They are not auditor-directed prompt injection, and the skill repeatedly limits use to explicit user requests or reported issues.

content.value_statement: Claude model migrations can break prompts, API settings, and platform model names. This skill gives Codex and Claude Code a focused checklist for moving supported Claude projects to Opus 4.5.
actual_capabilities: Identifies supported source models: Sonnet 4.0, Sonnet 4.5, and Opus 4.1.
Provides target Opus 4.5 model strings for Anthropic API, Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Azure AI Foundry.
Guides removal of an unsupported 1M context beta header during migration.
Documents the Opus 4.5 effort beta and example configuration values.
Suggests prompt adjustments for tool overuse, over-engineering, code exploration, frontend quality, and thinking sensitivity.
Instructs the assistant to summarize model string and prompt changes after migration.
limitations: It does not migrate Haiku 4.5 models.
It is guidance only and does not include executable migration scripts.
Prompt snippets should be applied only when the user reports the matching issue.
Model strings and beta names may require verification against current provider documentation.

use_cases.title: Upgrade API Integrations
use_cases.description: Update Claude model identifiers and beta headers in an application that calls Anthropic-compatible APIs.

use_cases.title: Tune Production Prompts
use_cases.description: Adjust existing prompts after an Opus 4.5 migration when users observe tool overuse or excessive implementation scope.

use_cases.title: Review Multi-Cloud Model Names
use_cases.description: Map Opus 4.5 identifiers across Anthropic API, Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Azure AI Foundry before release.

prompt_templates.title: Check Migration Scope
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project for Claude model strings that can be migrated to Opus 4.5. List the files and explain which models are supported.

prompt_templates.title: Update Supported Model Strings
prompt_templates.prompt: Migrate this codebase from supported Claude Sonnet or Opus 4.1 model strings to Opus 4.5. Do not change Haiku models. Summarize every file changed.

prompt_templates.title: Adjust Reported Prompt Behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: The Opus 4.5 migration caused unnecessary tool calls. Review the relevant prompt and soften only the tool-triggering language that causes overuse.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Multi-Provider Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this repository for Anthropic API, Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Azure AI Foundry Claude model usage. Propose a precise Opus 4.5 migration plan with risks and verification steps.

output_examples.input: A service uses Sonnet 4.5 through Bedrock and includes a 1M context beta header.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the Bedrock model name, recommends the Opus 4.5 Bedrock identifier, flags the unsupported beta header, and summarizes the required edits.

output_examples.input: A migrated prompt now calls tools too often.
output_examples.output: The assistant points to overly forceful tool-triggering instructions and suggests calmer wording while preserving the original prompt structure.

output_examples.input: A frontend prompt produces generic designs after migration.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends adding the frontend quality guidance only to the relevant design prompt section and explains the expected behavior change.

best_practices: Verify current provider model names before changing production configuration.
Apply prompt snippets only for observed behavior changes, not as a default migration step.
Summarize every model, beta header, and prompt change after the migration.
anti_patterns: Do not migrate Haiku model identifiers with this skill.
Do not append every prompt snippet to every system prompt.
Do not remove functional prompt content while adding Opus 4.5 adjustments.

faq.question: Does this skill run migration code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides migration guidance and examples for an assistant to apply with normal user approval.

faq.question: Which source models does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Claude Sonnet 4.0, Claude Sonnet 4.5, and Claude Opus 4.1 migrations to Opus 4.5.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Haiku migrations?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly says not to migrate Haiku models.

faq.question: When should prompt snippets be added?
faq.answer: Add them only when the user requests them or reports the specific behavior described by the snippet.

faq.question: Can it help with cloud provider model names?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists Opus 4.5 model strings for Anthropic API, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Azure AI Foundry.

faq.question: Should production teams verify the listed beta names?
faq.answer: Yes. Provider model names and beta flags can change, so teams should verify them before production release.
